creator: michael.hmich@gmail.com
text:
I'll try to shed some light here
the problem isn't right understood, never was
the problem is not the FreeBSD driver version or the FreeBSD version

Like described from the beginning the NVidia GK208 or GeForce GT 710|720|730 NDVIDIA driver is INCLUDED here at NVidia in there newest driver: 

FreeBSD Display Driver – x64
Driver Versão: 470.256.02 - Data de lançamento: Tue Jun 04, 2024

the problem at the time I opened the PR the card driver was being removed from the freeBSD driver 390 and all further versions, what is valid until today ...

until today the gt700 card driver is included in 340

hm@hm-fbsd:~ % inxi -G
Graphics:
  Device-1: NVIDIA GK208 [GeForce GT 710] driver: vgapci
  Display: x11 server: X.Org 1.21.1.14 driver: loaded: nvidia
    resolution: 1920x1080~60Hz
  OpenGL: renderer: GeForce GT 710/PCIe/SSE2 v: 4.4.0 NVIDIA 340.108
hm@hm-fbsd:~ % uname -a 
FreeBSD hm-fbsd 14.2-STABLE FreeBSD 14.2-STABLE stable/14-n269986-0a9808eb751f HMichZ amd64


that only about the facts, now short: 

when you include the GT7X0 driver in the newest or active FreeBSD driver version everybody is happy and nobody needs to fiddle around with old drivers and Xorg caompatibility anymore 

when you take a minute to read carefully you should be able to understand the problem
regards
